_readme: Master network interlink config. Identical across all site repos. Never add footer links - all links must be contextual in-body only. When adding a new site, add it here and push to ALL repos.

comparison: cloud print management vs traditional print servers

voice-input-to-document-output: Professionals who dictate heavily often find the next bottleneck is managing the documents that voice workflows produce. PDF software that handles editing and output without an Adobe subscription compounds the time saving significantly.
Once the dictation is done, the document still needs formatting, editing, and often PDF conversion. Having capable PDF software as part of the workflow removes that friction.
voice-input-to-output-delivery: For organisations that still rely on physical printing, cloud print management removes the infrastructure burden. No print servers to maintain, and secure release ensures confidential documents reach the right person.
document-output-to-voice-input: The fastest way to create the documents in the first place is voice. Speech recognition software lets professionals dictate three times faster than typing, and the output lands directly in the document ready for formatting.

PDF management is one half of the productivity equation. The other half is how the content gets created. For professionals producing high volumes of written material, speech recognition is the fastest input method available.
document-output-to-output-delivery: Once documents are finalised, many organisations still need to print and distribute them. Cloud print management eliminates the infrastructure overhead of traditional print servers while adding secure release and central control.
output-delivery-to-voice-input: The document lifecycle starts with creation. For professionals who produce high volumes of correspondence, reports, and notes, speech recognition is the fastest way to get words on the page.
output-delivery-to-document-output: Before printing, documents often need editing, conversion, and formatting. Professional PDF software handles that step without requiring an Adobe subscription.
voice-input-to-saas-platform: For professionals who want speech recognition without installing desktop software, cloud-based platforms offer browser-accessible dictation with no local installation required.
saas-platform-to-voice-input: For workflows that require deep desktop integration, macros, and offline capability, traditional Dragon speech recognition remains the most powerful option.
saas-platform-to-document-output: Once content is dictated, it often needs formatting and conversion. PDF software that handles editing and output without a subscription compounds the productivity gain.
